name: Package Airfoil as vendor artifact
description:
Difficulty: easy-medium.

Scope:
- Package Airfoil from official vendor artifacts because no official public source is available.

Implementation guidance:
- Use official vendor release metadata and artifact URLs.
- Prefer the repo's standard Darwin binary-app packaging path.
- Add sources.json and updater.py using first-party version metadata.

Done when:
- A reproducible vendor-artifact package can replace the Homebrew cask.

name: Fix Darwin crate2nix Tauri env propagation for opencode-desktop
description: The crate2nix attempt for opencode-desktop is currently blocked on Darwin-specific Tauri metadata propagation under buildRustCrate. While unblocking argus system builds, we found that tauri and tauri-plugin build-script outputs depend on transient build/source paths, some generated DEP_TAURI_* identifiers are not valid shell exports, cargo metadata like cargo:dev must survive across derivations, and permission-files payloads contain nested source paths that also need rewriting. The immediate system-closure fix is to keep using the previous buildRustPackage-based package definition for opencode-desktop. Follow-up work should make the checked-in Cargo.nix workflow viable on Darwin without regressing the existing package behavior. Acceptance criteria: darwinConfigurations.argus.pkgs.opencode-desktop builds via crate2nix on Darwin, the resulting package matches current app behavior, and argus system builds remain green.

name: Package Logi Options Plus as vendor artifact
description:
Difficulty: hard.

Scope:
- Package Logi Options+ from official vendor artifacts.

Implementation guidance:
- Treat device integration, privileged components, and background services as first-class constraints.
- Use official vendor release metadata where available and document any limitations or exception handling.

Done when:
- There is a documented package path or a clearly explained blocker/exception.

name: Package Freelens from source
description:
Difficulty: medium.

Scope:
- Build Freelens from the public upstream source repository.

Implementation guidance:
- Use upstream source rather than release bundles.
- Package according to the actual Electron/Node build workflow from source, including dependency lockfiles and native build steps as needed.
- Prefer a repo package under packages/ unless an overlay is clearly the better fit.

Done when:
- Freelens builds from source with Nix and is ready to replace the Homebrew cask.

name: Port closed-source Homebrew packages as vendor artifacts
description:
Scope: packages with no official public upstream source should be handled as prebuilt vendor artifacts and wired into the nixcfg packaging/update system.

Packages in scope:
- 1password
- airfoil
- antigravity
- arc
- claude
- claude-code
- cleanshot
- comet
- docker-desktop
- figma
- framer
- google-drive
- linear-linear
- lm-studio
- logi-options+
- loom
- nordvpn
- warp@preview

Implementation guidance:
- Prefer the repo-managed Darwin binary packaging pattern instead of ad hoc activation logic. Start by checking whether the upstream artifact is a DMG, ZIP, PKG, standalone binary, or another format.
- For DMG-style apps, prefer the existing helper pattern around overlays/_lib/helpers/darwin-apps.nix and mkDmgApp.
- If an upstream artifact is not a DMG, create the smallest reusable helper or package expression needed for that artifact type rather than forcing it through the wrong abstraction.
- Store vendor URLs and hashes in sibling sources.json files when the package participates in the repo update pipeline.
- Add updater.py modules that resolve versions and URLs from official vendor feeds such as Sparkle appcasts, vendor JSON manifests, release feeds, or other first-party metadata.
- Register new packages consistently with the repo discovery/constraint system in packages/registry.nix when needed.
- Preserve passthru.macApp metadata and install-mode semantics so nixcfg.macApps system application wiring continues to work.
- Validate with the narrowest package-specific build or eval first; only escalate to broader Darwin checks when needed.

Operational caveats:
- docker-desktop, google-drive, logi-options+, and nordvpn likely need extra scrutiny because privileged helpers, login items, VPN/filesystem integration, or mutable system state may make them poor fits for a pure app-bundle install. Record explicit constraints or exceptions instead of hiding that complexity.
- claude-code is not a GUI DMG app; package it according to the actual upstream artifact shape rather than assuming mkDmgApp applies.
- warp@preview must stay on the preview channel rather than being normalized to stable.

Good in-repo references:
- packages/raycast
- packages/conductor
- packages/sculptor
- overlays/vscode-insiders
- packages/wispr-flow

Done criteria:
- Each package in scope has an implementation task or completed package port using official vendor artifacts and update metadata.
- Any package that cannot be cleanly represented in the normal binary-app pattern has its constraints and exception handling documented explicitly.

name: Finish Zed crate2nix migration
description:
Complete the in-progress crate2nix packaging for zed-editor-nightly so local iteration and CI benefit from per-crate caching. There is already a draft implementation in packages/zed-editor-nightly/ with checked-in-style artifacts: default.nix, Cargo.nix, crate-hashes.json, normalize_cargo_nix.py, and README.md. The goal is to turn that draft into a working Darwin package that is faster to iterate on than the upstream flake/crane-based build.

Keep the repo pattern already used by Codex and Goose:
- checked-in Cargo.nix
- rootSrc = patchedSrc
- centralized crate overrides
- reuse upstream bundle metadata and assets where possible

Acceptance criteria:
- nix build for darwinConfigurations.argus.pkgs.zed-editor-nightly succeeds without linking
- the resulting zed binary smoke-tests cleanly, at least with --help
- any remaining blockers or unavoidable follow-up work are documented explicitly
result: Finished the Darwin crate2nix packaging for zed-editor-nightly in the working tree. Replaced the macOS app icon generation step that depended on sandbox-unavailable sips/iconutil with Nix-provided imagemagick plus libicns/png2icns in packages/zed-editor-nightly/default.nix. Verification: nix build .#darwinConfigurations.argus.pkgs.zed-editor-nightly --no-link succeeded and produced /nix/store/vq8pmf2156d2hnzxajs09a4nkkp0rbm9-zed-editor-nightly-unstable-8a7daea; the bundled app binary at Applications/Zed Nightly.app/Contents/MacOS/zed --help succeeded; the packaged CLI at bin/zed --help succeeded; file -L on the generated icns confirmed a Mac OS X icon file. Note: libicns still prints non-fatal warnings during icon generation, but the build completes and the icon artifact is valid.

name: Improve heavy Rust build caching with crate2nix
description: Track crate2nix adoption for the repo's heaviest Rust-heavy packages. The immediate priority is finishing the Zed crate2nix migration to improve rebuild times. Follow-up Rust candidates should be recorded here so they are not lost while Zed is the focus. Success for this initiative means Zed lands with a working high-cacheability crate2nix build, and any remaining worthwhile candidates are captured with enough context to revisit later.

name: Package KeepingYouAwake from source
description:
Difficulty: easy-medium.

Why this is early:
- Public upstream source is available.
- Native macOS app, but much smaller in scope than the heavier desktop apps.

Scope:
- Build KeepingYouAwake from official source rather than repackaging the release app.

Implementation guidance:
- Use the upstream source repo and native macOS build tooling.
- Prefer a real source build over DMG repacking.
- Ensure the packaged result integrates cleanly with the repo's Darwin app handling.

Done when:
- The app builds from source and can replace the Homebrew cask cleanly.

name: Package Yaak beta from source
description:
Difficulty: medium-hard.

Scope:
- Build Yaak from its public upstream source while preserving the requested beta channel semantics.

Implementation guidance:
- Use the official upstream repository/tags/commits from the beta channel.
- Do not normalize the package to stable.
- Package according to the upstream desktop build stack from source rather than using vendor binaries.

Done when:
- A source-built beta-channel package exists or the exact channel-pinning strategy is clearly documented.

name: Package Claude Desktop as vendor artifact
description:
Difficulty: easy-medium.

Scope:
- Package the Claude desktop app from official vendor artifacts.

Implementation guidance:
- Use the official first-party release feed rather than ad hoc scraped URLs.
- Treat this as a normal vendor desktop app package with the repo's Darwin app conventions.
- Add sources.json and updater.py for the official release metadata path.

Done when:
- The Nix package tracks the official desktop app artifact and can replace the Homebrew cask.

name: Migrate Homebrew installs to Nix packaging and updates
description:
Goal: replace the nixcfg Homebrew-managed formulae and casks with Nix-native packaging and update flows, then remove the Homebrew layer after parity is reached.

Current policy decision:
- If official upstream source is publicly available, build from source with Nix.
- Only packages with no official public source should be handled as prebuilt vendor artifacts.

Current inventory split from audit:

Source-available packages (build from source):
- mole
- codeedit
- freelens
- ghostty@tip
- gitbutler
- keepingyouawake
- macai
- macfuse
- pants
- rio
- signal@beta
- tailscale-app
- wave
- yaak@beta
- zen@twilight

No-official-public-source packages (package as prebuilt artifacts):
- 1password
- airfoil
- antigravity
- arc
- claude
- claude-code
- cleanshot
- comet
- docker-desktop
- figma
- framer
- google-drive
- linear-linear
- lm-studio
- logi-options+
- loom
- nordvpn
- warp@preview

Important caveats:
- gitbutler is source-available under FSL/fair-source rather than a standard OSI license; keep the source-first policy explicit unless repo policy says otherwise.
- macfuse, tailscale-app, and zen@twilight have public source but likely carry higher packaging complexity because of system integration, entitlements/signing, or browser-fork scale.
- ghostty@tip, signal@beta, and yaak@beta require channel-specific pinning and should not be silently normalized to stable.
- pants upstream source is public, even though the current Homebrew cask installs the scie-pants launcher artifact.

References in this repo:
- Binary Darwin app helper: overlays/_lib/helpers/darwin-apps.nix
- Existing binary-package examples: packages/raycast, packages/conductor, packages/sculptor, overlays/vscode-insiders
- Package discovery/constraints: packages/registry.nix
- Update framework: lib/update/updaters/, sources.json, updater.py

Acceptance criteria for this initiative:
- Each Homebrew-managed package has an explicit Nix migration path and implementation ticket.
- Source-available packages are tracked as source builds, not vendor binaries, unless a deliberate exception is recorded.
- Closed-source packages are tracked with official vendor artifact packaging/update flows.
- Once parity is achieved, the nix-homebrew/homebrew declarations can be removed cleanly.

name: Revisit other Rust-y crate2nix candidates after Zed
description:
Record the non-Zed Rust-ish packages that might be worth moving to crate2nix later, so we can stay focused on Zed now without losing the analysis.

Current recommendation set:
- opencode-desktop is the strongest follow-up candidate because it is a Rust/Tauri build currently using buildRustPackage plus Tauri git-dep hashing and sidecar wiring; per-crate caching would likely help
- scratch is a lower-priority maybe: also Tauri/Rust, but probably smaller so the maintenance tradeoff is less clear
- sentry-cli is probably not worth crate2nix because the current fetchCargoVendor approach is likely sufficient
- opencode itself is primarily Bun/JS-oriented, so the desktop backend is the real Rust target rather than the main package
- codex-desktop is a binary app, not a source Rust target
- gitbutler was previously investigated, but the repo history leaned toward crane or other approaches and later dropped the package from Nix, so it should not be treated as an active crate2nix target

Revisit this list only after Zed is in good shape.

name: Package 1Password as vendor artifact
description:
Difficulty: easy-medium.

Scope:
- Package 1Password from official vendor artifacts because no official public source is available.

Implementation guidance:

- Add sources.json and updater.py backed by first-party release metadata.

Done when:

name: Package Pants from source
description:
Difficulty: medium-hard.

Scope:
- Build Pants from its public upstream source rather than packaging only the scie-pants launcher artifact.

Key decision to record:
- Confirm whether strict source-build parity is the intended end state, versus packaging the upstream launcher for operational parity. The policy decision currently favors source-first because official source is public.

Implementation guidance:
- Start from the upstream source repository and its real build/bootstrap model.
- Do not assume crate2nix is the right primary tool just because Rust is present; this is a Python-centric project with additional moving pieces.
- Document the chosen packaging model explicitly in the task result.

Done when:
- There is a clear source-build package path for Pants, or an explicitly justified exception is recorded.

name: Package Framer as vendor artifact

Difficulty: easy-medium.

Done when:

name: Package CleanShot as vendor artifact

Done when:

name: Migrate opencode-desktop to crate2nix
description: Migrate opencode-desktop from buildRustPackage to a checked-in crate2nix workflow, following the repo patterns used by Codex, Goose, and Zed where they make sense. The package is a Rust/Tauri app and is the strongest remaining non-Zed crate2nix candidate in the repo. Goals: generate and check in Cargo.nix and any required support files, keep the package building on Darwin, preserve the existing desktop app packaging behavior, and improve rebuild ergonomics through per-crate caching. Acceptance criteria: nix build for darwinConfigurations.argus.pkgs.opencode-desktop succeeds, the packaged binary or app smoke-tests cleanly if practical, and any unavoidable follow-up work is documented.

name: Upstream Darwin optimiser skip for flat .app bundles
description: Local nixcfg now carries a patch that broadens Darwin store optimiser bundle skipping from only \.app/Contents/** to any nested \.app/** path, fixing EPERM when optimise/gc encounters flat app bundles such as expo-mcp's AutomationApp.app/_CodeSignature/CodeResources. Follow up by upstreaming the change to Nix (issue context: NixOS/nix#2229 and current optimise-store.cc Darwin workaround), and add or identify a regression test covering flat app bundle layouts on macOS.

name: Package Loom as vendor artifact
description:
Difficulty: easy-medium.

Scope:
- Package Loom from official vendor artifacts.

Implementation guidance:
- Use the official desktop update feed rather than undocumented URLs.
- Package it through the repo's standard binary-app/update path.

Done when:
- Loom is represented as a maintained vendor-artifact package with official update metadata.

name: Port source-available Homebrew packages as source builds
description:
Scope: every Homebrew-managed package whose official upstream source is publicly available should be packaged as a source build with Nix rather than as a vendor DMG/ZIP/Pkg.

Packages in scope:
- mole
- codeedit
- freelens
- ghostty@tip
- gitbutler
- keepingyouawake
- macai
- macfuse
- pants
- rio
- signal@beta
- tailscale-app
- wave
- yaak@beta
- zen@twilight

Implementation guidance:
- Prefer an existing nixpkgs source package first if it already matches the product/channel well enough. Use an overlay when a targeted version or channel override is the smallest correct change.
- If nixpkgs is missing the package or the upstream channel requirements differ, add a repo package under packages/<name>/default.nix unless overriding nixpkgs semantics is clearly better in overlays/.
- Use upstream source tags/commits/tarballs or other official source artifacts, not vendor desktop bundles.
- Match the build strategy to the upstream stack instead of forcing one pattern everywhere:
  - Rust/Tauri: cargo or crate2nix where it meaningfully improves repeatability and dependency control.
  - Electron/Node/Bun apps: package from source with the relevant JS lockfile/dependency workflow plus native build steps as needed.
  - Swift/AppKit/SwiftUI apps: use native macOS build tooling; avoid re-wrapping release DMGs if the source build is viable.
  - Go apps: use buildGoModule or another source-first approach appropriate to the upstream layout.
  - Browser forks or very large apps: expect extra bootstrap/toolchain work and record any justified exceptions explicitly.
- Preserve channel intent: ghostty@tip, signal@beta, and yaak@beta must stay on their requested channels rather than being normalized to stable.
- Regenerate machine-maintained artifacts such as Cargo.nix, crate-hashes.json, uv.lock, bun/npm dependency manifests, or sources.json when the chosen packaging approach requires them; do not hand-edit generated files.
- Validate with the narrowest relevant checks first, then add heavier package-specific builds only when needed.

Per-package notes:
- mole: likely a straightforward source build from the public upstream repo.
- codeedit and keepingyouawake: native macOS apps; prefer real source builds over DMG repacks.
- freelens, macai, wave, yaak@beta: desktop-app builds with JS/native toolchains; package from source, not vendor artifacts.
- ghostty@tip: source exists, but the requested tip channel means commit/build pinning must be explicit.
- gitbutler: source exists under FSL/fair-source terms; keep license considerations visible during implementation.
- macfuse and tailscale-app: source exists, but system integration, signing, installer, entitlement, or kernel/system-extension concerns may dominate the work.
- pants: upstream source is public even though Homebrew currently installs the scie-pants launcher; decide whether strict source-build parity or launcher parity is the better end state, and document that choice.
- zen@twilight: source exists, but this is effectively a Firefox-fork-scale package with twilight-channel requirements.

Done criteria:
- Each package in scope has an implementation task or completed package port that keeps the source-first policy intact.
- Any exception to source-first packaging is explicitly justified and recorded in the task history.

name: Package Claude Code as vendor artifact
description:
Difficulty: medium.

name: Package mole from source

Implementation guidance:

Done when:

Done when:

name: Package MacAI from source

name: Package Wave from source
description:
